title: Highlights: Morandé La Capilla El Cabernet de Ránguil 2021 Itata, Chile
summary: This is quite special. It’s from 130 year old ungrafted vines planted on degraded granite soils, with a red clay
content:
This is quite special. It’s from 130 year old ungrafted vines planted on degraded granite soils, with a red clay matrix and a high presence of quartz. Aged 16 months in used French oak. It’s not normal to find old vine Cabernet in Itata: most of the old vines here are País, Cinsault or Muscat.

Morandé La Capilla El Cabernet de Ránguil 2021 Itata, Chile
13% alcohol. This is supple and elegant with delicious, fresh, slightly gravelly blackcurrant fruit with a chalky, mineral texture and nice focus and precision. There’s a faint hint of that Chilean pastille-like fruit character. It’s a refined, understated, elegant expression of this grape variety showing beautiful poise and balance. Supple and massively drinkable, but with some seriousness, too. I much prefer this style to the very ripe, full-on Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon style. 94/100

title: Highlights: Cecilia Wines Cinsault 2025 Piekenierskloof, South Africa
summary: Cinsault was historically a hugely important red grape for South Africa. Often, red ‘varietals’ from fancy grapes like Cabernet Sauvignon
content:
Cinsault was historically a hugely important red grape for South Africa. Often, red ‘varietals’ from fancy grapes like Cabernet Sauvignon would have a good dollop of Cinsault in them. It’s a variety that copes with heat really well, making fresh, lighter-style red wines. There’s less of it now than there used to be, but still quite a bit, and its time has come.

Cecilia Wines Cinsault 2025 Piekenierskloof, South Africa
13% alcohol. Cerina van Niekerk was previously the winemaker at Piekenierskloof. Supple and sappy with lovely red cherry and strawberry fruit. There’s a real focus to this wine, with some silkiness and sweetness to the fruit, with a touch of redcurrant, but also showing a bit of tannic crunch and good acidity. This is a beautiful expression of Cinsault, so nuanced and floral. 93/100 (£22 Stone Vine and Sun)

title: Excursions off the Riesling trail: what else can the Finger Lakes do?
summary: This was a seminar at the FLXcursion event that I’ve just come back from. The goal was to explore the
content:
This was a seminar at the FLXcursion event that I’ve just come back from. The goal was to explore the things that the Finger Lakes can do other than Riesling, its star grape variety.

The panel consisted of three: Tara Q Thomas, Jamal Rayyis and Lisa Granik. They pointed out that in 1900 there were 20 000 acres of grapes in the Finger Lakes region and they sold 7 million bottles of sparkling wine each year. The grape industry was thriving, and it was huge. Then prohibition came along, and after the war tastes changed. California was doing well, and the European sensibility in wine spread, and people changed their tastes away from the hybrid fare of New York State’s wine regions.

Then along came Konstantin Frank. At age 63 he started his own winery and he changed the view on whether or not it was possible to grow vinifera here. The industry is still reinventing itself, and there are still a lot of hybrids here. But we are now seeing a return to interesting hybrid grapes to make wines with personality.

These are the wines we tasted.

Konstantin Frank Rkatsiteli 2025 Finger Lakes, New York
A relatively neutral grape variety with thick skins and tending to low acidity. Light and open with nice citrus and white peach fruit as well as some citrus. Fruity and appealing with good texture and weight. Easy and expressive. 90/100

Casreli Marani Amidasturi 2022 Kakheti, Georgia
This is a blend of Kakhuri Mtsvane, Muskaturi Rkatsiteli and Rkatsiteli. Four months in qvevri on skins, no stems. Amber colour. Nice structure here with some tannins under the appealing, aromatic peach and pear fruit with a touch of baked apple. Has nice weight and structure. Really appealing, finishing firmly tannic and dry, but with such lovely open aromatics. 91/100

Standing Stone Saperavi 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Deeply coloured, this has beautiful fresh black cherry and blackberry fruit with some nice grippy tannins and some acidity. Juicy but with some richness on the mid-palate. Chalky, grainy structure with a spicy bite on the finish. 93/100

Mukado Saperavi 2024 Kartli, Georgia
This is pure and nicely balanced with some tannic bite. Lovely berry and black fruits with some chalkiness and a bit of spicy detail on the finish. Really appealing. 93/100

Fox Run Grüner Veltliner 2025 Finger Lakes, New York
Nice weight here with pear and citrus fruit showing good texture, finishing fresh. Juicy and layered with a slight saltiness and a hint of white pepper. This is textural and appealing with nice layers of flavour. 92/100

Fritsch Ried Steinberg Grüner Veltliner 2023 Wagram, Austria
This is bright and textured with nice weight to the pear and citrus fruit with some herbal hints, some yellow fruit, and then some pepper notes on the finish. Nice weight to this wine. 92/100

Heart & Hands Esoterra 2023 Finger Lakes, New York
56% Auxerrois, 23% Aligoté, 21% Petite Arvine. This is supple, easy and fruity with sweet pear and table grape notes, a touch of creaminess and balanced acidity. Fruity and easy to drink. 89/100

Ambrositch Gemischter Satz Ringelspiel 2021 Wien, Austria
12 varieties. Fresh, fine, transparent and easy. Mandarin, lemon and pear fruit with a touch of watermelon. Light and expressive with real drinkability. 90/100

Keuka Lake Vineyards Skin-Fermented Vignoles 2021 Finger Lakes, New York
Two weeks on skins. Full yellow/gold. Good acidity and some tannic structure as well as apple and pear fruit. Nice weight here: it’s floral but there’s some spicy weight, finishing slightly sour. 90/100

Fio Wines Mosel Glou Glou 2024 Mosel, Germany
Pinot Blanc and Müller Thurgau. A month on skins. Cloudy with some nice mineral reductive character as well as fresh pear and citrus fruit. Juicy and expressive with freshness and nice texture. So drinkable and delicious. 93/100

Usonia Good Spirit 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
65% Chancellor, 20% Baco Noir, 10% Traminette, 5% Aromella. Some spicy bacon character here as well as juicy cherry and berry fruits. Fresh and supple with some pepper on the finish. Juicy and expressive. 90/100

Vignoble de Rêveur Rien ne Bouge 2023 Vin de France
60% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Gris, 10% Pinot Blanc. This is from Alsace. Lighter in colour, this is supple and smashable with red cherry and strawberry fruit. Such elegance and freshness with beautiful fruit. Fresh with nice acidity but also showing some silky red fruit character. Very fine. 94/100

Ravines Agricolae Keuka Red 2022 Finger Lakes, New York
Noiret and Cabernet Franc. Concentrated, textural and pepper with nice spicy detail and a bit of grip. Really distinctive and quite delicious. Grainy structure. 90/100

Marco Sara Schioppettino Friuli Colli Orientali 2023 Italy
Bright, peppery and vivid with some strawberry and red cherry. Textural and expressive, this is lighter in style but also has some mid-palate warmth. Textural and expressive. 92/100

title: Weingut Frank, one of the stars of Austria’s Weinviertel region
summary: Website: https://www.weingutfrank.at/englishhome Frank is a family winery that’s been going since 1724. They’re currently farming 23 hectares of vines, and
content:
Website: https://www.weingutfrank.at/englishhome

Frank is a family winery that’s been going since 1724. They’re currently farming 23 hectares of vines, and they are based in the far north east of the region.

They specialize in Grüner Veltliner, but also like to work with Riesling, which is 10% of their vineyard. ‘Grüner Veltliner is our most important variety, but we love Riesling,’ says Katrin Frank, who I tasted with at the 2026 edition of Vievinum. These are impressive wines.

Weingut Frank Grüner Veltliner Kalk Loess 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
11.5% alcohol. Fresh, linear and bright with a core of citrus fruit. Mineral and expressive with nice intensity and a hint of pepper. Stylish. 91/100

Weingut Frank Grüner Veltliner 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
12.5% alcohol. Linear, bright and fresh with nice tension, and some peppery notes. Good citrus drive with a crystalline quality to the fruit. Pure. 92/100

Weingut Frank Grüner Veltliner Ried Johannisberger 2024 Weinviertel, Austria
Single vineyard with loess soils. Stainless steel ageing on full lees. Textural with notes of white pepper, showing good focus and intensity. Peppery and fruity. 93/100

Weingut Frank Grüner Veltliner Ried Hoher Weg 2024 Weinviertel, Austria
13.5% alcohol. This is a smaller part of Johannisberger. It’s a windy spot, and soils are loess. 600 litre barrels. Textured, fine and delicate with some depth. White pepper, pear and citrus. Rounded, but finishes slightly salty. 94/100

Weingut Frank Gruner Veltliner Baumgartleiten 2023 Weinviertel, Austria
Limestone soils, raised in 600 litre barrels. Fresh, linear and textured. Bright with nice energy, and some notes of pepper as well as crystalline lemons. Lovely focus and refinement. 94/100

Weingut Frank Riesling Herrnbaumgarten 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
A village-level wine. Linear, bright and fruity with lovely texture. So expressive with nice fresh fruit but also good texture. Really good. 93/100

Weingut Frank Pinot Blanc Herrnbaumgarten 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
Linear, bright and fruity. Textural and expressive with nice fresh fruit but also some nice texture. 92/100

Weingut Frank Zweigelt 2024 Weinviertel, Austria
Lovely floral aromatics here. Really pure and vivid, and so inviting. Nice fruit, with a pure, joyful personalitu and good balance. 93/100

Weingut Frank Ried Krainholz Pinot Noir 2023 Wienviertel, Austria
The soils are a mix of loess and limestone. Supple and elegant with fine red cherry fruit as well as some strawberry, with fine-grained structure. Such a lovely wine. 94/100

title: The wines of Weingut Kurz, Weinviertel, Austria
summary: Website: https://www.weingutkurz.at/ Eric Kurz (pictured above) is the third generation in this family winery, and makes 80% white wines and 20%
content:
Website: https://www.weingutkurz.at/

Eric Kurz (pictured above) is the third generation in this family winery, and makes 80% white wines and 20% red, working alongside his parents Paul and Erika. Grüner Veltliner is the main variety here. They are based in the centre of the region. I tasted with Eric at VieVinum in May 2026.

Kurz Welschriesling 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
13% alcohol. Aromatic, with a floral, fresh fruity drive. Fresh, stony and bright on the palate, showing nice weight to the dry fruit. Driven and expressive. 90/100

Kurz G’Mischta 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
12% alcohol. A blend of Grüner Veltliner and Riesling with a dribble of Sauvignon. Aromatic, fruity and expressive with nice juicy citrus and a bit of grapiness. Bright and slightly pithy. 90/100

Kurz Ried Edelsbergen Grüner Veltliner 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
13% alcohol. Fresh and juicy with nice brightness to the citrus fruit. Light and very fruity, showing good precision and nice weight. Understated. 91/100

Kurz Ried Hainthal Grüner Veltliner 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
Juicy, supple and bright with nice energy, and a slight peppery edge to the layered citrus fruit. Pure with good acidity and some freshness. 91/100

Kurz Ried Hochberg Sauvignon Blanc 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
12.5% alcohol. Delicately herby. Subtle, stony and bright, with nice purity. Has a lightness to it. 88/100

Kurz Ried Kirchbergen Gelber Muskateller 2025 Weinviertel, Austria
13% alcohol. Bright and fruity with nice grippy detail and some citrus drive. Juicy and lively with nice herbal hints. 89/100

Kurz Grüner Veltliner Ried Hungherm Reserve 2022 Weinviertel, Austria
14% alcohol. Aged in new French oak for 18 months. Full yellow in colour, this is bold and rich with notes of melon, pear and peach. Nicely intense with good weight. Low-ish acidity, with notes of honey and toast in the mix. Bold style. 91/100

Kurz Roesler Ried Edelsbergen Weinviertel, Austria
14% alcohol. There are just 400 hectares of Roesler (a red grape) in Austria, and this is a blend of 2023 and 2024 aged in American oak. Deeply coloured, this is bold, rich, spicy and dense with vivid spicy notes alongside the sweet black fruits. Satisfyingly broad. 90/100

title: A trio of impressive Pet Nats from UK winery Westwell
summary: Westwell are nailing Pet Nat. Everyone makes one, and sometimes they are accidentally good. But to do it well consistently
content:
Westwell are nailing Pet Nat. Everyone makes one, and sometimes they are accidentally good. But to do it well consistently takes some skill. These three are lovely. The Westwell wines are also the best packaged in the UK, thanks to Galia Pike’s intricate illustrations.

Westwell Petulant Nature Chardonnay 2025 Kent, England
Some sweet lemon at the core of this wine which shows rich mid-palate texture, and some herbal hints – basil and lavender – and then bright citrus. It’s quite fresh and effervescent, and gushes a tiny bit (but manageably) on opening. The Chardonnay shows itself with a citrus dominance, and there’s brightness and energy, and some chalky texture and then bright lemon and mandarin on the finish. It’s joyful but there’s also a bit of seriousness here, albeit not too much. Such presence to this Pet Nat. 92/100

Westwell Naturally Petulant 2025 Kent, England
12% alcohol. Chardonnay, Pinot Meunier and Pinot Noir. This is the first fruit from Westwell’s new plantings at Birling Estate. Picked 29 September at 20.7 Brix and a TA of 10.8 g/l. Wild yeast fermentation, then bottled before the finish on 16 October. Hand disgorged. This is very pale pink, with wonderful aromas of sweet raspberry and strawberry with a hint of sweet apple, leading to a precise palate with lovely textured raspberry, cranberry and ripe pear fruit, with a hint of herbal character and lovely bass and treble, without too much middle. It’s joyful, sweetly fruited, but has a nice bite on the finish keeping things very fresh and alert. This is really impressive. 92/100

Westwell Petulant Nature Pink 2025 Kent, England
11.5% alcohol. Ortega and Regent. Fermented in stainless steel but bottled before fermentation was complete Pet Nat style. Then hand disgorged. Deep pink in colour, this is just beautiful, with pure, sweet cherry and raspberry fruit and nice brightness. It has amazing drinkability, and is pleasure in a glass. 91/100

title: Highlights: Gallo of Sonoma Frei Ranch Vineyard Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon 2001
summary: Pleasantly surprised by this older Californian Cabernet. Gallo of Sonoma Frei Ranch Vineyard Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon 2001 Dry Creek
content:
Pleasantly surprised by this older Californian Cabernet.

Gallo of Sonoma Frei Ranch Vineyard Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon 2001 Dry Creek Valley, Sonoma, California
13.8% alcohol. At almost 25 years old, it’s great to be able to try this wine in its maturity. It’s still very much alive. Aromatic with spice-laden blackcurrant fruit on the nose. There’s a bit of sweet oak here (it spent 24 months in barrel), but also lots of fruit, and some dried herbs and a bit of lift. Nicely evolved. In the mouth this is fresh and concentrated with sweet berry and blackcurrant fruit, some sweet acidity, a touch of orange peel and some chalky, gravelly, woody savoury notes. There’s also a hint of cooked tomato. It reminds me of some of the great old Hunter reds that aged beautifully, but had some citrus/acid notes sticking out a bit at the end, giving a juicy character. Very stylish, still structured, still plenty of fruit, and drinking very well now. 93/100

title: Tenerife winery Altos de Trevejos starts a new phase with Borja Pérez as winemaker
summary: Website: https://www.altosdetrevejos.com/ We crossed from the north, via the Teide national part, to the south of Tenerife. Here you can
content:
Website: https://www.altosdetrevejos.com/

We crossed from the north, via the Teide national part, to the south of Tenerife. Here you can find something rather unusual for this island: a relatively large vineyard. It’s in the Albona appellation (one of five in Tenerife) which goes up to a lofty 1600 m, and the 20 hectares of vines are at 1300 m. We met with owner Henrique Alfonso, and Borja Pérez who has just started making the wines with the 2025 vintage.

Compared to the green north, it’s very dry here. Typically they get 200 mm of rain a year, and usually the only treatment the vines need is some elemental sulfur.

The soils here are arenas negras – black sands from a volcanic eruption in 1706. Grape varieties are Listán Blanco, Albillo Criollo, Verdelho, Malvasia Aromatico, Listán Prieto, Baboso Negro and Vijarejo Negro.

Borja has got involved with the viticulture in his debut year as winemaker, changed the approach (no more blending, all the varieties kept separate) and he feels that he still needs to fine tune the viticulture of the reds. But I thought all the wines were pretty spectacular.

These were all cask samples. The wines will be bottled in a few months. 2025 was a warm, dry year with 200 mm rain. 2026, an atypically damp year, has seen 400 mm rain so far.

Altos de Trevejos Listán Blanco 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
Two 500 litre barrels, one new. Bright, linear, stony and pure. Lovely citrus fruit drive. Mineral, crystalline and quite saline, with high acidity. This is beautiful. 95/100

Altos de Trevejos Verdelho 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
One new 500 litre barrel. Some smoky notes on the nose. Crystalline palate with smoky grapefruit character, some pear, and some bright citrus notes. Juicy, grainy and quite fine. 94/100

Altos de Trevejos Albillo Criollo 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
This variety comes from La Palma. Fermented and aged in a 500 litre Stockinger barrel. Full yellow in colour. Pungent and vibrant on the nose. The palate is crystalline, spicy and vivid with pear, peach and melon fruit. It’s rich but there’s also freshness, and some lovely salinity alongside the chalky texture. 95/100

Altos de Trevejos Malvasia Aromatica 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
Borja Pérez thinks that this is the best of the white varieties in the Canaries. Smoky and spicy, with a mineral nose. Intense, lively, stony on the palate with lovely citrus drive. Delicate structure under the fruit with amazing presence. So intense and crystalline. 95/100

Altos de Trevejos Listán Prieto 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
Fermented whole bunch, 20 day maceration, then MLF in barrel. Vivid, juicy cherry and raspberry fruit with nice brightness and some crunch. This is fresh and supple with green hints in the background, some raspberry crunch, and nice tension. 94/100

Altos de Trevejos Baboso Negro 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
This is still quite a rare variety and most wineries only have a bit. Trevejos have a significant 2.5 hectare holding. This is supple with nice concentration of ripe black cherry fruit. This is really expressive with some flesh and then some chalky tannins, finishing with some grip. Lovely. 94/100

Altos de Trevejos Vijarejo Negro 2025 Albona, Tenerife, Spain
This is another name for Sumoll. Nice intensity here: vivid and grippy with lovely black fruit intensity and good structure. Fresh but firm. 93/100

title: At Osmote, Ben Riccardi is making some of the Finger Lakes’ best wines, and is shining a light on modern hybrids as well as vinifera varieties
summary: Website: https://www.osmotewine.com/ Ben Riccardi (pictured above) is doing some special things at Osmote. On my first visit to the Finger
content:
Website: https://www.osmotewine.com/

Ben Riccardi (pictured above) is doing some special things at Osmote. On my first visit to the Finger Lakes back in 2017 I managed to taste a few of his wines, and I was impressed. He’d just started out (first vintage was 2014) and at the time was making wines at Damiani. Since then he’s bought a property on the east side of Seneca Lake and also moved into his own winemaking facility.

He’s also focusing on hybrids. As well as making excellent Chardonnay, Cabernet Franc, Pinot Noir and Riesling, Ben is working very smartly with hybrids, which are an important part of the Finger Lakes story. Last year he even planted some: 2 acres of Aromella and 2 acres of Marquette. They are much more sustainable to farm, and the results can be excellent if a lot of care is taken.

Osmote TTYL Dry white 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
70% Cayuga White, 30% Melody. This is pure and delicious with pear and lemon as well as some fine spicy detail. Nice texture and depth. 92/100

Osmote Chardonnay 2024 Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
12.5% alcohol. Barrel fermented, wild yeast. Spicy and detailed with some mealy richness. Pear and peach with some spicy citrus. Nice depth here with lovely balance and complexity. 93/100

Osmote Riesling 2023 Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
Citrus and pear fruit with nice depth, showing nice depth with some apple notes. Rich and textural with nice crystalline fruit. Juicy and expressive. Some richness. 91/100

Osmote Pinot Noir 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Lovely cherry and plum fruit. Floral with some herby detail. Silky and expressive with fine grained detail. There’s elegance and poise here: a lovely Pinot. 94/100

Osmote Cabernet Franc 2024 Seneca Lake, Finger Lakes, New York
Such focus and elegance with raspberry and cherry fruit with fine grained tannins. Lovely grip here. Has some elegance as well as a bit of structure. 94/100

Osmote Bici Dechaunac 2024 Finger Lakes, New York
Spicy, vivid, grippy and a bit wild. Firm tannins. Spice and tar and nice acidity. An impact wine with a lot of flavour. Juicy finish. 90/100

title: Video: regenerative viticulture at Champagne Perrier-Jouët
summary: Jamie Goode investigates the regenerative viticulture plots in the Ambonnay vineyards of Champagne Perrier-Jouët. This project has been going since
content: Jamie Goode investigates the regenerative viticulture plots in the Ambonnay vineyards of Champagne Perrier-Jouët. This project has been going since 2021, and now more than half of their vineyards are farmed this way, because it is making a noticeable (and positive) difference to the base wines.
